project.name: Autosport Manager 2027
project.description: A PC-based sports management game focused on team management and race tactics

target.audience_details: Experienced gamers familiar with F1, F2, other motorsports management games

scope.focus: Team management & real-time race simulation with 24 AI drivers
core_features: Strategic race simulation (24 AI drivers, distance-based progression, weather-affected outcomes)
Tactical race planning (pit strategy, tire compound selection, driver instructions: push/defend/save)
Season progression (24 races, 10-month calendar with testing sessions)
Vehicle setup & development (aerodynamics, chassis, power unit research trees)
Career progression system (driver morale dynamics, team reputation, sponsor relationships)
Live race monitor (standings, telemetry, pit strategy visualization, incident log)
Dynamic weather system (rain intensity, track temperature, tire degradation modifiers)
Tire strategy system (soft/medium/hard compounds, degradation curves, temperature windows)
excluded_v1: Direct car control (player orders, does not steer/brake/accelerate)
3D track recreation with detailed graphics (simplified 2D/schematic acceptable)

team.current_mode: Full Development (Design Mode focus for pre-production)

phase_1_preproduction.gdd: 100+ pages covering all game systems, UI, formulas, AI behavior
phase_1_preproduction.tdd: Technical architecture, engine choice, platform details, tools
phase_1_preproduction.prototype: Playable build of 1 race weekend with basic UI
phase_1_preproduction.roadmap: Weekly breakdown for main production phase

engine.recommendation: Unity 2022.3 LTS (best for UI-heavy management game with responsive data visualization)
engine.rationale: UIToolkit for performant real-time graphs, built-in physics unnecessary (distance-based sim only), Steam integration, asset ecosystem

performance_targets.notes: Management game: 30 FPS acceptable (not real-time action). Simulation at 20 Hz sufficient for pit strategy calculations. UI refresh 10 Hz for telemetry updates.

high_priority: Overtake probability tuning produces unrealistic race outcomes (too few or too many passes per race)
AI makes illogical pit strategy decisions (e.g., pits too late, wrong compound choice)
Save/load file corruption causing player progress loss
UI performance degrades with 24-car standings + live telemetry graphs
Licensing: F1/FIA may prohibit use of real team names, drivers, logos
medium_priority: Per-track polyline content pipeline (24 circuits from open GPS sources) blocks Visual Tier Level 2 delivery; per-circuit licensing must be validated
Player doesn't feel strategic depth (all decisions feel obvious, no tension)
Memory leaks over long play sessions (frame rate degradation after 2+ hours)
Modding community expectations (player-created cars, teams, championships)
low_priority: Platform-specific optimization (AMD GPU compatibility, Intel vs AMD CPU variance)

risk_management.critical_decision: RESOLVED 2026-04-25: Hybrid architecture (lap-tick base + probabilistic overtake + lightweight incident model) with Visual Tier Level 2 (2D track map, dot-rendered cars). Single-threaded sim with mandatory SnapshotBuffer abstraction. Non-deterministic by design with dev-only seed override for QA. Track polylines sourced from open GPS data. See documentation/ARCHITECTURE_DECISION_PHYSICS_VS_SIMPLIFIED.md (status: ACCEPTED) and documentation/technical/01_ARCHITECTURE_OVERVIEW_REVISED.md (sections 1.6.1, 1.6.2, 1.7, 1.9, 1.14).
